header_subtitle: Connect AI assistants like Claude, Cursor, VS Code, and Codex to your DS-APM data via the Model Context Protocol. Authenticate from your MCP client with a service-account API key.
step1_description: Add DS-APM to your MCP client. Use a one-click install where available, or copy the config for manual setup. On first connect, the client will open a DS-APM authorization page - use the instance URL and API key from step 2.
auth_description: On first connect, your client opens a DS-APM authorization page asking for two values:
helper_admin: Create a service account, then add a new key inside it - paste that key into the API Key field.
helper_non_admin: Only admins can create API keys. Ask your workspace admin for a key with read access, then paste it into the API Key field.
use_cases_title: What you can do with it
use_case_1: Ask your AI assistant to investigate a spiking error rate.
use_case_2: Debug a slow service by walking through recent traces.
use_case_3: Summarize an alert and suggest likely root causes.
use_case_4: Generate dashboards or queries from a natural-language description.
manual_setup_hint: Or copy the config below for manual setup.
fallback_title: MCP Server is available on DS-APM
fallback_body: Users can follow the docs to setup the MCP server against their DS-APM instance.
